steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses: “The sand at Balos Beach in Greece is a more unusual shade” and “deposits of crushed coral and other organic matter lend the sand a unique pink hue.” These two independent clauses must be properly separated by punctuation.

steps.title: Apply the semicolon rule for independent clauses
steps.content: Two independent clauses require a semicolon to separate them. The word “though” here functions as a postpositive adverb (similar to “still” or “yet”) that closes the first clause. When a postpositive adverb ends an independent clause, it must be followed by a semicolon before the next independent clause begins: IC, though; IC. The other three choices either omit the required semicolon (A), misuse a colon (B), or incorrectly treat “though” as parenthetical material (C). That matches choice D — D is correct.
